threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it’s cool to walk your dog without a coat in winter. Everyone else’s pup is sporting a stylish fleece by now.
Heard a busker on the 50 last night belting out old Amy Winehouse - crowd went wild, felt like we were at a Brixton gig instead of stuck in traffic.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted an old lady knitting by the bus stop while a group of kids were breakdancing next to her. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how people here will cheer when the tram finally arrives on a snow day? It's like a mini-celebration for public transport!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their reaction to BART delays; they roll their eyes, while newcomers look genuinely confused and start checking Google Maps.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the bus stop with a ukulele while a pigeon danced nearby. Only in SF does rush hour feel like a street performance.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists miss that the morning mercados are just as vibrant as La Boqueria but way more authentic. Try the ones on Travessera!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they still struggle with the 'll' sound when ordering at a café—it's like hearing them try to speak a secret language.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'Overground' instead of the 'Ginger Line' when bragging about your commute.
Caught the 59 last night and a guy was playing saxophone in the back. He was amazing, but the driver looked ready to kick him off at any moment.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Walking past the open-air market on Maybachufer, I realized I wasn't just a visitor; I was part of the chaos of the fruit vendors haggling.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you enjoy a late-night döner while listening to an impromptu techno set in the park. It’s a uniquely chaotic slice of Berlin life.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they navigate around the BART delays without breaking a sweat, while newcomers are just staring at the app in confusion.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy arguing with the Muni driver about the AC not working while the bus was packed. Classic Mission moment – sweat and sass all around.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí; they miss the tiny bars where locals debate football late into the night, that’s where the real vibe is.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still trying to figure out how to navigate the labyrinthine streets without pulling up Google Maps every two minutes.
